doom.driver: Iran says it fired missiles at US warship to prevent it entering Hormuz.
doom.blurb: MISSILES MEET 'PROJECT FREEDOM' IN THE STRAIT
doom.summary: Iran just fired missiles at a US Navy destroyer — the kind of escalation that turns 'diplomatic stalemate' into 'accidental world war.' Meanwhile, Trump launched 'Project Freedom' to guide stranded ships through Hormuz, which Iran promptly threatened to turn into Project Wreckage. With oil already at $126 and India burning extra coal, the economic blast radius of this strait is now a full-spectrum global crisis.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Iran firing missiles at a US warship in Hormuz is a direct military engagement — one misfire away from a whole new chapter of this war.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Trump's unilateral 'Project Freedom' naval escort mission bypasses Congress again while Fed chair nominee Warsh rattles markets with unorthodox Fed independence views.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: AI misallocating healthcare resources in Kenya and outperforming ER triage doctors in the same week — capability racing ahead of accountability, as usual.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Hantavirus kills three on a cruise ship in the Atlantic; WHO confirms one case with five more suspected — small outbreak, large psychological footprint post-COVID.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Ukraine's Sky Map drone-intercept AI being repurposed for Gulf operations is a quiet reminder that battlefield AI proliferation has no off switch.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, silhouette composition, pre-dawn Strait of Hormuz — the narrow chokepoint geography dominates the frame, the rocky ochre cliffs of the Iranian coastline rising as a jagged black mass along the upper left, the Musandam Peninsula's limestone teeth barely visible as a dark smear along the opposite horizon, the dark teal-green water filling the lower two-thirds of the frame with choppy pixel waves catching deep crimson light from an unseen source low on the horizon — everything rendered as stark black silhouettes against a bruised sky of dark crimson bleeding into charcoal purple, a sliver of violent orange-red glow separating sea from sky at the horizon line — in the mid-ground, the unmistakable angular silhouette of an Arleigh Burke-class destroyer, its flat-faced SPY-1D radar panels and VLS deck hatches recognizable against the glow, hull cutting the dark water, American flag a tiny pixel detail at the stern — beyond it, the enormous black rectangle silhouettes of VLCC supertankers clogging the shipping lane, dwarfing everything — on the Iranian cliffside in the far left foreground, the dark outline of a hardened coastal battery structure with launch rails angled seaward, camouflage netting draping the bunker edge — low across the water surface, two thin white contrail streaks barely above the waves converging toward the center frame, firelight reflections shimmering in broken crimson fragments on the dark chop below — oppressive, high contrast, no text, no antialiasing.

dawn.driver: Powerful AI finds 100+ hidden planets in NASA data including rare and extreme worlds.
dawn.blurb: AI UNLOCKS A HUNDRED NEW WORLDS
dawn.summary: Even as geopolitical crises dominate the headlines, humanity's curiosity reaches deeper into the cosmos. A new AI tool called RAVEN has combed through NASA's TESS mission data and confirmed over 100 previously hidden planets — including rare and extreme worlds we never knew existed. At a moment when Earth feels dangerously small and fractious, it's worth pausing on the fact that we just expanded our map of the universe.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: RAVEN's AI-driven planetary sweep of NASA TESS data confirms 100+ hidden worlds, demonstrating that space science continues accelerating regardless of earthly turmoil.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Canada's historic invitation to the European Political Community summit signals that new alliances are forming organically to fill the vacuum left by fractured old orders.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Rescuers defied warnings and carried a stranded humpback whale named Timmy by barge into the North Sea, racing against time in an act of sheer collective compassion.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Amsterdam has banned public advertising for meat and fossil fuels, a small but symbolically powerful municipal commitment to its own environmental targets.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: North Korean women footballers will make a rare trip to South Korea for a continental semifinal — sport quietly opening a door that diplomacy has kept shut for years.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Night scene, all light from artificial sources, deep shadows dominating. The vast cosmos fills the frame as an infinite night backdrop — an impossibly dense river of emerald-gold stars cascading across deep space like a glittering Milky Way band rendered in rich saturated greens and warm amber pixels. At the scene's heart, NASA's TESS satellite floats in deep space, its boxy silver-gold foil body and twin rectangular solar panel wings catching vivid golden light, four wide-angle camera eyes glowing outward, Earth a tiny blue-green crescent receding in the far dark distance. Dominating the upper canvas, a blazing amber-gold star burns with saturated warmth as a tiny silhouetted disc — a transiting exoplanet — crosses its surface, the classic dimming event rendered as a perfect dark circle against fierce orange-white fire. Surrounding the satellite, streams of luminous light curves cascade like emerald rain, thousands of thin glowing lines representing 2.2 million stars sorted by an unseen AI intelligence. In the lower foreground, three small human silhouettes sit at glowing monitors in a pitch-dark computational lab, their faces lit from below by cool blue-white screen glow contrasting against the warm gold and vibrant emerald of the cosmos above — dwarfed by the universe they are mapping, one small figure leaning forward in a moment of quiet, sacred wonder.
